name: San Bernardino
metaTitle: San Bernardino Accident Guide — Hospitals, Courts & Rights
metaDescription: Injured in San Bernardino? Find local emergency hospitals, courts, and understand your rights under California personal injury law.
description: San Bernardino is the county seat of San Bernardino County — the largest county in the contiguous United States by area — and serves as the eastern gateway to the Inland Empire. I-10 runs through the city carrying massive freight volume between the Ports of Los Angeles and Long Beach and the national rail network, making it one of the busiest commercial truck corridors in the Western United States. I-215 intersects the city from north to south, connecting San Bernardino to Riverside and beyond. SR-30 links the city to neighboring Highland and Redlands. SR-18, known locally as the Rim of the World Highway, climbs into the San Bernardino Mountains and is frequently the site of motorcycle, RV, and passenger vehicle accidents on its steep curves. Baseline Street and Highland Avenue are high-traffic urban arterials with significant pedestrian exposure near commercial districts and public transit stops. The Omnitrans bus network operates extensively through the city and may be a defendant in transit-related injury cases. Government claim deadlines apply strictly given the city's many public roads and sidewalks.

commonAccidentTypes: Freight truck accidents on I-10 between Inland Empire distribution centers and the ports
High-speed freeway collisions on I-215 at interchange merge zones
Mountain road accidents on SR-18 (Rim of the World Hwy) involving motorcycles and RVs
Pedestrian accidents on Baseline St and Highland Ave near bus stops
Intersection collisions on Waterman Ave and E St commercial corridors
Bicycle accidents on urban arterials near California State University San Bernardino
notableCorridors: I-10 — primary national freight corridor with extremely heavy commercial truck volume
I-215 — north-south freeway connecting San Bernardino to Riverside
SR-30 (Highland Freeway) — east connector to Highland and Redlands
SR-18 (Rim of the World Hwy) — mountain highway with steep curves and recreational vehicle traffic
Baseline St — urban east-west arterial with high pedestrian and commercial exposure
Waterman Ave — major north-south commercial corridor prone to intersection conflicts
localNotes: San Bernardino is in San Bernardino County. Accidents involving city or county property, sidewalks, or government vehicles require a government claim filed within 6 months of the injury date. San Bernardino Police Department traffic collision reports can be requested through SBPD Records. Omnitrans bus service is a potential defendant in transit-related injury cases throughout the city.
